name: Benevity Corporate Social Responsibility API
what_it_does: Benevity REST API for corporate social responsibility (CSR), employee giving, volunteering, and grants management. Enables AI agents to manage employee charitable giving and donation matching for corporate giving program automation, handle volunteer time tracking and opportunity management for employee engagement programs, access corporate grants management and foundation giving for strategic philanthropy workflow, retrieve cause discovery and nonprofit verification for giving program curation, manage giving campaign and challenge management for employee engagement, handle impact reporting and CSR analytics for sustainability reporting, access Spark (cause marketplace) integration for consumer cause marketing, retrieve nonprofit due diligence and charity vetting for donation compliance, manage giving account and disbursement workflow for donation processing, and integrate Benevity giving data with HRIS, payroll, and ESG reporting platforms.
use_cases: Automating donation matching from corporate giving agents
Managing volunteer programs from employee engagement agents
Processing corporate grants from philanthropy agents
Reporting CSR impact from ESG reporting agents
not_for: Small businesses without formal corporate giving programs
Individual charitable donors without employer giving context
best_when: A large corporation with a formal CSR program using Benevity wants AI agents to automate employee giving, donation matching, volunteering management, grants processing, and ESG impact reporting.
avoid_when: TAX DEDUCTIBILITY RISK: Automated charitable donation matching must verify recipient nonprofit 501(c)(3) status and IRS good standing; matching donations to non-compliant or sanctioned organizations creates corporate tax deductibility risk. OFAC sanctions compliance — automated donation processing must screen recipient organizations against OFAC SDN list; donations to sanctioned organizations create federal compliance violations. Corporate governance — automated grant approval above defined dollar thresholds must route to appropriate board/committee approval level; automated bypasses of grant approval authority create fiduciary governance issues.

auth.notes: Benevity uses OAuth 2.0. API access via Benevity partner program. Webhooks for giving events. REST API with JSON. Calgary, Alberta HQ (Canada). Founded 2008 by Bryan de Lottinville. Private (JMI Equity, General Atlantic). 1,000+ Fortune 1000 corporate customers. $10B+ donated through platform. CSR and employee engagement leader. Competes with YourCause (Blackbaud) and Bright Funds for corporate giving.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription; per-employee fee; transaction fees on donations
pricing.notes: Calgary, AB, Canada. Founded 2008. JMI Equity/General Atlantic-backed. 1,000+ Fortune 1000 customers. $10B+ donated. CSR market leader. Competes with YourCause and Bright Funds for corporate giving.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per partner integration

known_agent_gotchas: OFAC SANCTIONS COMPLIANCE: Automated donation disbursement must screen recipient nonprofits against OFAC SDN and OFSI lists; donation to sanctioned organization creates federal compliance violation for corporate donor

501(c)(3) verification — automated donation matching must verify recipient current IRS good standing; matching donations to organizations with revoked tax-exempt status creates corporate tax deductibility risk

Grant approval authority — automated grant approval must respect corporate delegation of authority; grants above threshold require board/committee approval; automated bypass creates fiduciary governance issue
PIPEDA Canadian privacy — Benevity serves Canadian employers and employees; employee giving and personal information is subject to Canadian PIPEDA privacy requirements

Anti-money laundering — large automated donation or grant disbursements may trigger AML reporting requirements; charitable giving programs have been used for money laundering; implement monitoring for unusual patterns
ESG reporting accuracy — Benevity impact data feeds ESG/sustainability reports filed with investors; automated data errors in giving and volunteering metrics create SEC ESG disclosure accuracy risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: Corporate CSR and giving. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/CA/EU. Employee giving and nonprofit financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate CSR platform. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/CA/EU. Employee giving and donation data.
